author: parithosh
content:
:ssz: pls ❤️
CL can't move away from ssz. EL can opt not to move to ssz, but if they move to something else then we are stuck supporting two things forever. Esp if ssz is marginally worse or better than anything else, then ease of maintenance should justify just using ssz.

author: somnergy
content:
The preprocessing that some reth provers do at the moment is JSON witness -> transform -> bincode serialize (rust built in).

We do JSON witness -> transform -> rlp serialize

Transform is necessary for flattening maps, for instance, and while we are at it, we arrange the nodes in a certain order.

There is but one escape from this if we propose an endpoint that does this all in one go. We are currently doing some research on this aspect and will put in to standardization.

Note that this is inescapable as a full node client team won't be prioritizing this task of optimizing for the guest input (they don't even want to allow some bits of pre-indexing in BALs). So, politically speaking, I don't know where this will land.

author: jsign
content:
> We are currently doing some research on this aspect and will put in to standardization.
Nice. Does this proposal involve to anotehr way to encode the mpt nodes part for the guest program input? (e.g. a flat vec with a particular order so mpt construction is more efficient)

author: kevaundray
content:
I think we can create an endpoint that gives data in the correct format? ie engine_newPayloadWithWitness

I do agree that there might be some transform needed, though I'd recommend that were first have it working e2e with the specs as a priority.

Initially, this would mean that the current debug_executionWitness would transform into the ssz format specified in the specs
